title: Vision & Image Paste
summary: Enables multimodal vision by letting users paste images from clipboard into the CLI or messaging platforms; automatically routes images to the model as native vision content or through an auxiliary vision tool for text-only models.

config_keys.description: Auxiliary vision model for text-only model image routing

slash_commands.purpose: Attach image from clipboard (most reliable method)
Install recommended VS Code/Cursor/Windsurf terminal keybindings for better paste parity

code_examples.caption: Vision content format sent to model

gotchas: If clipboard has ONLY an image (no text), use /paste — terminals cannot send binary image bytes directly
SSH remote sessions: clipboard tools read the remote server clipboard, not your local machine
WSLg clipboard bridge only supports BMP format — Hermes auto-converts with Pillow or ImageMagick
/terminal-setup writes LOCAL IDE keybindings — do not run from an SSH session
full_text:
# Vision & Image Paste

Hermes Agent supports **multimodal vision** — paste images from clipboard directly into the CLI and ask the agent to analyze, describe, or work with them.

## How It Works

1. Copy image to clipboard
2. Attach via `/paste` command or Ctrl+V/Cmd+V
3. Type question and press Enter
4. Image appears as `[📎 Image #1]` badge
5. On submit, sent to model as vision content block

Multiple images can be attached. Press Ctrl+C to clear. Images saved to `~/.hermes/images/` as timestamped PNGs.

## Paste Methods

### `/paste` Command
Most reliable fallback. Type `/paste` and press Enter — Hermes checks clipboard for image and attaches it.

### Ctrl+V / Cmd+V
Layered flow: text paste first, then native clipboard/OSC52 fallback, then image attach if clipboard resolves to image.

## Platform Compatibility

| Environment | `/paste` | Cmd/Ctrl+V | Notes |
|---|:---:|:---:|---|
| macOS Terminal / iTerm2 | ✅ | ✅ | Uses osascript |
| Linux X11 | ✅ | ✅ | Requires `xclip` |
| Linux Wayland | ✅ | ✅ | Requires `wl-clipboard` |
| WSL2 (Windows Terminal) | ✅ | ✅ | Uses powershell.exe |
| VS Code / Cursor (local) | ✅ | ✅ | Use /terminal-setup |
| SSH terminal | ❌ | ❌ | Remote clipboard inaccessible |

## Platform Setup

- macOS: no setup needed (uses `osascript`); optionally `brew install pngpaste`
- Linux X11: `sudo apt install xclip`
- Linux Wayland: `sudo apt install wl-clipboard`
- WSL2: no setup (uses `powershell.exe` automatically)

## SSH Workarounds

1. Upload via `scp` and reference by path
2. Use a URL — agent can use `vision_analyze` on any URL
3. X11 forwarding with `ssh -X`
4. Send via Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp (platform handles upload)

## Image Routing

| Your model | What happens |
|---|---|
| Vision-capable | Sent as real pixels using native image content format |
| Text-only | Routed through `vision_analyze` auxiliary tool — text description injected |

Hermes auto-detects model capabilities and picks the right path. Configurable via `auxiliary.vision`.
